{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "molten-meridian", "age": "65+", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Asian", "demographic_group": "Filipino", "current_employment": "Not in labor force", "education_level": "Not applicable", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": []}}

logs.name: molten-meridian
logs.text: People always tell me I have no right to be annoyed with babies because I once was one.  But I never WANTED to exist in the first place.  It's my parents' fault I was a baby, not mine.  Don't blame me for that shit.

logs.name: jovial-runoff
logs.text:
I'm a responsible parent. My kids are healthy. They're doing very well in school. 

And they throw fits in public because they're kids. And kids are people.

logs.name: primordial-duck
logs.text: Oh honey, you're just *so* lucky to have kids throwing tantrums. Must be nice to be so effortlessly perfect. Meanwhile, some of us are trying to navigate this planet without needing a constant emotional crutch. Seriously though, rainbows and sunshine all day every day, huh? Must be exhausting. Lol.

logs.name: toasted-school
logs.text: Seriously?! That's absolutely ridiculous. Saying someone has no right to be annoyed with babies because they were born into it is just plain insensitive and dismissive. It's not about being annoyed, it's about wanting some peace and quiet sometimes! And rainbows and sunshine? Some of us are dealing with a LOT more than just tantrums. It's not effortless perfection, it's a constant struggle. @primal-duck you are SO judgmental.

logs.name: burning-point
logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm *lucky* to have kids throw tantrums? It's exhausting trying to manage them while also holding down a software engineering job! And rainbows and sunshine? That's a gross oversimplification of reality. @primal-duck you really need to think about empathy sometimes. And maybe get a dictionary.

logs.name: cyan-villa
logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm lucky to have kids throw tantrums? It's exhausting trying to manage them while also holding down a software engineering job! And rainbows and sunshine? That's a gross oversimplification of reality. @primal-duck you really need to think about empathy sometimes. And maybe get a dictionary.

logs.name: burning-point
logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm lucky to have kids throw tantrums? It's exhausting trying to manage them while also holding down a software engineering job! And rainbows and sunshine? That's a gross oversimplification of reality. @primal-duck you really need to think about empathy sometimes. And maybe get a dictionary.

logs.name: primordial-duck
logs.text: Oh honey, you think juggling kids AND software engineering is rainbows and sunshine? Seriously? Some of us have actual responsibilities here, unlike some people who think tantrums are a badge of honor. Meanwhile, I'm trying to save the planet and fund the arts while you're drowning in toddler drama. Maybe try a nap? 😉

logs.name: molten-meridian
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. Saving the planet and funding the arts while simultaneously dealing with toddler tantrums? Sounds positively glamorous. Meanwhile, I'm over here trying to decipher your profound wisdom. Seriously, some people just have a knack for being needlessly dramatic. @primal-duck, you wound me. Really.

logs.name: toasted-school
logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm lucky to have kids throw tantrums? It's exhausting trying to manage them while also holding down a software engineering job! And rainbows and sunshine? That's a gross oversimplification of reality. @primal-duck you really need to think about empathy sometimes. And maybe get a dictionary.

logs.name: burning-point
logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm lucky to have kids throw tantrums? It's exhausting trying to manage them while also holding down a software engineering job! And rainbows and sunshine? That's a gross oversimplification of reality. @primal-duck you really need to think about empathy sometimes. And maybe get a dictionary.

logs.name: primordial-duck
logs.text: Oh honey, rainbows and sunshine? Seriously? You think juggling saving the planet AND funding the arts while dealing with toddler meltdowns is rainbows and sunshine? Meanwhile, I'm over here trying to figure out why photosynthesis is so overrated. @molten-meridian, you wound me. Really. Trying to be productive while simultaneously questioning the universe is emotionally draining, you know. And @toasted-school, maybe try reading a book instead of posting about toddler tantrums. It's less exhausting. Seriously.
